tools.name: submit_urls
tools.description: Submit a list of URLs to be checked. Returns a job_id that can be polled via get_job_status or fetched via get_job_results. For up to ~200 URLs this tool waits for completion (up to 60 seconds) and returns the results directly; for larger jobs it returns early with job_id and the agent should poll.

tools.name: get_job_status
tools.description: Get the current status and progress of a job.

tools.name: get_job_results
tools.description: Fetch paginated results for a job. Each result has the URL, HTTP status code (null if unreachable), response time in ms, and an error message if the check failed.

tools.name: get_usage
tools.description: Return the current credit balance for this API key.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: submit_urls, get_job_results · Private data: get_usage · Public sink: submit_urls.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: submit_urls, get_job_results · Private data: get_usage · Public sink: submit_urls

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
